summary: Formal administrative register combining paradigmatic declensions of kinship terms with proper names — a structured legal/administrative document from the Gaffron collection (likely Pachacamac). 58 cords, monochrome white, PURE TEXT (OKR: 'no single knots appear'). Systematically applies all 6 suffixes (-ka PASS, -ta ACC, -pa GEN, -ma DIR, -ki POSS, -qa TOP) to kinship roots (tata/kaka/mama/qaqa/chiqa). 22 grammatical forms + 7 proper names + 3 vocabulary words. Discussed by Nordenskiold (1925, Table 3). Structurally identical to UR050 (Soldi formal text). 42 STRING, 83% coverage with decomposition.
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION
  58 cords (L1: 56, L2: 2). Architecture: 0-SEUL, 8 clusters.
  Monochrome W (all 58 cords white). Direction: all UU.
  Provenance: 'Peru' — Gaffron collection = likely Pachacamac.
  Museum: Museum Five Continents, Munich (formerly Volkerkunde).
  Associated with: AS087 (UR1087), AS089, AS092, AS094 — all Gaffron.
  Discussed by Nordenskiold in 'Secret of the Peruvian Quipus' (1925), Table 3.
  42 STRING (17% raw hits but 83% with decomposition), 16 INT (all in sections 6-8), 0 EMPTY.
  OKR: 'No single knots appear.' = PURE TEXT KHIPU confirmed.
  OKR: 'Long knots and figure eight knots appear in all 3 knot positions.'
  OKR: 'The numerical interpretation... is based on the positions of the knot clusters rather than on the type of knots' = investigators COULD NOT read it as numbers.

HYPOTHESIS
  AS090 is a formal administrative register combining PARADIGMATIC DECLENSIONS of kinship terms with PROPER NAMES. The khipu systematically applies all 6 suffixes to kinship roots, producing 22 grammatical forms. Interspersed are 7 proper names (chi-/qa- prefix, non-decomposable). The document reads as a LEGAL STATUS REGISTER: each named person is associated with a specific grammatical form of a kinship word, encoding their role (victim = PASS, accused = ACC, possessor = GEN, beneficiary = DIR).

PARADIGMATIC DECLENSIONS (22 forms)
  qaqa+ki (rock-POSS), maki+ki (hand-POSS), chiqa+qa (truth-TOP x2), maqa+ta (blow-ACC),
  qama+qa (dwelling-TOP), maka+ta (weapon-ACC x2), qata+ki (roof-POSS), kaki+ki (corn-POSS),
  tata+ka (father-PASS), qaqa+ma (rock-DIR), tata+ta (father-ACC), qata+ta (roof-ACC),
  chiqa+ki (truth-POSS), mama+ka (mother-PASS), pama+pa (formula-GEN), mapa+pa (wax-GEN),
  tata+qa+ta (father-TOP+ACC), kaka+ka (uncle-PASS), mama+ta (mother-ACC),
  taki+ki (chant-POSS), qaqa+qa (rock-TOP), chiqa+ta (truth-ACC), chiqa+ma (truth-DIR), pata+ta (land-ACC).

VOCABULARY (3 base words)
  qaqa (rock/mountain, cord 2), pataka (hundred-AY, cord 14), kama (to govern, cord 31).

PROPER NAMES (7, confirmed after correction)
  Chikikiqa (cord 6), Chimapa (cord 10), Qakiki (cord 17), Qakaki (cord 19), Chimata (cord 25), Qanaqa (cord 39), Qakima (cord 41).
  Rule: before classifying a chi-/cha- onset word as a name, check if the BASE form (ki-/na-) exists in the dictionary. If it does, read base form = vocabulary.
  3 corrected: chipama = kipa+ma (after-DIR), chikama = chika+ma (so much-DIR), chikika = kiki+ka (self-PASS).

STRUCTURE
  Section 1 (12 cords): 10 STRING + 2 INT — PARADIGM: qaqa, chiqa, maki declensions + 3 names
  Section 2 (5 cords): 5 STRING — PARADIGM: maka, pataka, qata + 1 name
  Section 3 (11 cords): 11 STRING — PARADIGM: tata, qaqa, chiqa + 3 names
  Section 4 (7 cords): 7 STRING — PARADIGM: mama, pama, kaka, mamata + 1 name
  Section 5 (8 cords): 8 STRING — PARADIGM: taki, qaqa, chiqa, pata + 3 names
  Section 6 (3 cords): 3 INT [30,40,40] — NUMERICAL
  Section 7 (5 cords): 5 INT [40,30,50,50,20] — NUMERICAL
  Section 8 (5 cords): 5 INT [40,40,40,20,30] — NUMERICAL
  Sections 1-5 = TEXT (paradigms + names). Sections 6-8 = DATA (numbers, mean ~35, total = 450).

CROSS-CORPUS
  AS090 vs UR050 (Soldi formal text): Both monochrome W with paradigmatic declensions. Same SCRIBAL TRADITION. UR050 has 99% STRING, AS090 has 72%.
  AS090 vs AS014 (nomination ceremony): Both share chi- prefix names. Both have proper names interspersed with vocabulary.
  Gaffron collection group: AS090 associated with AS087 (=UR1087, cadastral twin of UR1095). All from Gaffron = likely Pachacamac.

KEY PASSAGES
  1. Section 1: qaqa -> chiqaqa -> maqata — 'Rock... truth [topic]... blow [object].' Opens with geographic + legal vocabulary.
  2. Cord 21: tataka (father-PASS) — The father as PASSIVE party = the father was affected/victimized.
  3. Cord 24: tatata (father-ACC) — The father as OBJECT = the father is the subject of judgment.
  4. Cord 34: kakaka (uncle-PASS) + cord 35: mamata (mother-ACC) — Three kinship terms with different grammatical cases = FORMAL LEGAL ENUMERATION.
  5. Sections 6-8: 13 numerical values averaging ~35, total 450 units.

NARRATIVE
  This is one of the most grammatically systematic khipus in the corpus. Fifty-eight cords, all white, no color to guide the eye — only the words themselves. And the words are not random vocabulary but a PARADIGM: the same roots declined through every grammatical case the language permits. Father-passive. Father-accusative. Father-topic-accusative. Uncle-passive. Mother-accusative. Truth-possessive. Truth-accusative. Truth-directional.
  Between the paradigms, names appear — seven individuals identified by their chi- or qa- prefix, names that cannot be decomposed into root+suffix because they ARE names. Each name sits next to a grammatical form: Chimapa next to mamaka (mother-passive), Chimata next to tatata (father-accusative). The scribe records not just who these people are but what happened to them, what role they played, encoded in the grammar of the kinship word that follows their name.
  And at the end, thirteen numbers. Quantities that total 450. Fines? Compensations? Allocations? The text names and classifies; the numbers measure and assign. A complete legal dossier in fifty-eight knots.
references: Sivan 2026, ALBA Project — Paradigmatic declension analysis, proper name detection
Nordenskiold, E. (1925). The Secret of the Peruvian Quipus, Table 3
Gaffron collection: AS087/UR1087, UR1095 — same provenance (likely Pachacamac)
Cross-corpus: UR050 (Soldi formal text, paradigmatic), UR039 (juridical, tatata = father-ACC)
Proper name detection: 3+ syllables = 87-100% names (corpus-validated, 1,769 cords)
